steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left side: “the units sa and shesep were used to measure area and ___” is an independent clause (subject “units,” verb “were used”).

Right side: “neither unit is commonly used today” is an independent clause (subject “unit,” verb “is”).

The word “respectively” appears in the answer choices as a transition element.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be joined with a semicolon (not a comma alone), so Choice C correctly uses “length, respectively;” — a comma to set off the transition word and a semicolon to join the two clauses.

Choices A misplaces the semicolon before “respectively,” B omits the necessary punctuation, and D uses only a comma to join the clauses.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
